entities.name: AGRICULTURE
entities.description: The engine for poverty reduction and economic development in developing nations, employing a large portion of the population in South Asia and Sub Saharan Africa

entities.name: POVERTY REDUCTION
entities.description: The process of reducing the number of people living in poverty, driven by agriculture in developing nations

entities.name: ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The process of improving the economic well-being and wealth of a region, driven by agriculture

entities.name: SOUTH ASIA
entities.description: A region where agriculture employs over 50% of the population

entities.name: SUB SAHARAN AFRICA
entities.description: A region where agriculture employs over 50% of the population

entities.name: GROSS DOMESTIC PRODUCT
entities.description: A measure of the total value of goods and services produced in a region, significantly contributed to by agriculture in SA and SSA

entities.name: DRYLANDS
entities.description: Agricultural lands vulnerable to droughts, occupying approximately 60% of the global land area

entities.name: DROUGHTS
entities.description: Environmental threats of various intensities that affect agricultural lands in drylands

entities.name: SEMIARID REGIONS
entities.description: Regions within drylands where drought threats are more pronounced

entities.name: ARID REGIONS
entities.description: Regions within drylands where drought threats are more pronounced

entities.name: LIVELIHOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having secure means of living, which is critical for millions of rural people in dryland agriculture

entities.name: INNOVATIONS
entities.description: Technological changes and new methods in agriculture that are currently slow and patchy in dryland sectors

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The efficiency of agricultural production, which remains low due to low adoption of technologies

entities.name: RESOURCE DEGRADATION
entities.description: The decline in the quality of natural resources resulting from low adoption of innovations

entities.name: POVERTY
entities.description: A persistent state of deprivation resulting from low productivity and resource degradation

entities.name: SMALLHOLDER FARMERS
entities.description: Rural farmers in developing countries whose livelihoods are the target of improvement efforts

entities.name: EXTENSION MODEL
entities.description: The traditional top-down technology-driven system for delivering agricultural knowledge

entities.name: INNOVATION SYSTEMS
entities.description: Approaches to innovation that emphasize collective dimensions, linkages, and interaction among multiple actors

entities.name: INNOVATION PLATFORMS
entities.description: Multistakeholder arrangements serving as mechanisms to enhance agriculture innovations and strengthen stakeholder capacity
